items.q: How many semitones are in a perfect fifth?
items.why: Students count fifth as 'five steps' but the name refers to letter NAMES (1-2-3-4-5), not semitones. C to G spans 5 letter names but 7 semitones (C-C#-D-D#-E-F-F#-G).

items.q: Which orchestral family does the saxophone belong to?
items.misconception: Brass (because it is made of brass).
items.why: Students classify by material. Instrument families are defined by SOUND PRODUCTION: woodwinds use a reed or air across an edge. The sax uses a reed, so despite the brass body, it's woodwind.

items.q: What happens in the recapitulation of sonata form?
items.correct: Both subjects return, but the second subject is now in the tonic key.
items.why: Students miss the KEY RESOLUTION. The exposition put subject 2 in a contrasting key; the recapitulation brings it back to the tonic, resolving the tonal tension - this is the dramatic point of sonata form.
Correct answer: Beats per minute. “Bass per measure” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Pop songs are typically what BPM?
items.why: Correct answer: 120. “20” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many BPM is a typical pop song?
items.why: The real figure is 120. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
